text: The estimated average protein requirement for endurance athletes after a high-volume training day is 1.65 g/kg/day based on indicator amino acid oxidation methodology.
The recommended protein intake for endurance athletes after a high-volume training day is 1.83 g/kg/day (upper 95% CI of the estimated average requirement).
Protein requirements for endurance athletes determined by indicator amino acid oxidation are higher than the current sport nutrition recommendation of 1.2-1.4 g/kg/day based on nitrogen balance.
The general RDA of 0.8 g/kg/day protein is insufficient for endurance athletes on higher volume training days.
Whole body phenylalanine flux remains stable across a wide range of protein intakes (0.2-2.8 g/kg/day) in endurance athletes, indicating metabolic adaptation in amino acid turnover.

A controlled pre-study diet of 1.4 g protein/kg/day combined with 10 km and 5 km training days followed by a 20 km run represents sufficient metabolic stress to elevate protein requirements above sedentary levels.
